tavily.answer: The latest conflict in northern Mozambique involves Islamist attacks that have led to the suspension of the Mozambique LNG project by TotalEnergies and its partners, causing an estimated $2 billion in overruns. Mozambique has contested these cost estimates. Additionally, Mozambique has taken steps to tighten control over its mining sector by implementing a new law requiring 15% state ownership in all mining ventures and local processing of minerals. In a separate incident, five Mozambican nationals died in anti-immigrant attacks in South Africa. Mozambique has also trained its first cohort of 30 certified drone operators to respond to natural disasters, marking a shift in its approach to disaster management.
